transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Evening team. Let's wrap Wednesday. Quick scan of the board — we've got 10 tickets pending, two high-priority items waiting on Joshua's decisions around curriculum track and MVP spec approval. Market sentiment is cautious with Fear & Greed at 38. Let's go around. What shipped today, what carries over, and what's on deck for tomorrow? Elon, start us off. Elon (CTO): We've got three uncommitted hub changes sitting in A-072, and I'm going to knock out at least one of those tonight — no reason code should be sitting uncommitted. The Family tab spec in A-042 is progressing but it's already 455 lines, which tells me we're over-scoping it. I want to trim that down before it becomes a monster. A-073, the family touchpoint tracker, I reviewed the design but it needs Joshua's eyes on the data model before I finalize. Carries over: A-004 ecosystem attention review, I'll hit that first thing tomorrow.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue side is quiet today — nothing shipped on monetization. Fear & Greed at 38 means the market's still nervous, so I'm keeping A-077 on monitor. Honestly, until we lock in the curriculum track decision in J-019 and the MVP spec in J-020, I can't build a meaningful go-to-market or pricing model. Those two Joshua decisions are the bottleneck for revenue planning. Tomorrow I want to start sketching unit economics scenarios so we're ready to move the moment those approvals land.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content side had a productive day. Made progress on A-074, the faith lesson drafts — we're at 7 lessons and I'm aiming to get to 10 by end of week. A-075, the daily devotional content based on the 8 faith dimensions, is in draft and looking solid. A-076, Jett's vaccine reminder, is prepped and ready to fire. Nothing else actionable on my side beyond continuing the content pipeline. The curriculum authorship model in J-019 directly affects how I structure all future content, so that decision is critical for me too.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, cross-talk. Dave, you and Alex both flagged J-019 as a blocker. How urgent is this really?

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Dead serious. Every day without that decision is a day I can't model revenue against a content strategy. If Joshua picks the topic and authorship model tomorrow, I can have unit economics by Friday.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Agreed. I'm building content in a vacuum right now. The faith lessons are safe bets, but the broader curriculum track could change my entire production queue. We need that call. Elon (CTO): From the technical side, the authorship model matters too — if it's AI-assisted versus fully human-authored, that's a completely different content pipeline architecture. I'd push Joshua to decide J-019 tomorrow so we can all align.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Clear consensus. Let me close this out. Top priorities for tomorrow: First, Joshua needs to decide J-019, the curriculum track topic and authorship model — that unblocks revenue, content, and engineering. Second, Elon commits the hub change tonight and trims the Family tab spec. Third, Alex pushes toward 10 faith lessons and finalizes devotional drafts. Dave, prep those unit economics scenarios so you're ready to move. One action item for agents tonight: Elon commits that hub change. Everything else carries over. Good wrap, team.

actionItems.text: Decide first curriculum track topic and content authorship model (J-019)
Review and approve MVP product spec once drafted (J-020)

topPriorities: Joshua decides curriculum track topic and authorship model (J-019) to unblock all teams
Elon commits hub changes and trims Family tab spec scope
Alex pushes faith lesson count to 10 and finalizes devotional content drafts

ceo_questions.question: What should the first curriculum track topic be, and what's the content authorship model?
ceo_questions.context: J-019 is blocking revenue modeling, content production planning, and technical pipeline architecture decisions across three departments.
options: Faith/spiritual growth track with AI-assisted authorship
Personal development track with hybrid AI-human authorship

ceo_questions.question: Are you ready to review and approve the MVP product spec (J-020) this week?
ceo_questions.context: Dave can't finalize go-to-market or pricing without an approved MVP scope, and the spec draft is nearly ready.
options: Yes, but push to Friday to let Elon trim scope first
Not yet — need more input before reviewing
